primary_argument: NVDA's +4.20% move today on normal volume, paired with upstream chain strength (AVGO +5.66%, TSM +1.39%) and the emerging sovereign AI demand layer from China's $295B AI infrastructure push, signals sustained hyperscaler and non-hyperscaler GPU allocation cycles that will drive Blackwell revenue acceleration through 2026-2027.
supporting_evidence: NVDA up 4.20% to $204.12 on normal volume (0.92x 30-day average) while entire upstream chain moves higher: AVGO +5.66%, TSM +1.39%, SMH +2.97%. China's $295B AI data center strategy explicitly targets domestic high-tech stocks and creates a new demand vector independent of US hyperscaler capex cycles. Meta's Canada data center expansion and Beijing's approval process for H200 chip purchases signal sustained global infrastructure deployment momentum.
key_assumption: Hyperscaler capex cycles remain elevated through 2026-2027 AND sovereign AI demand from China, Singapore, and other non-US governments materializes as a sustained, incremental demand layer that does not cannibalize but supplements hyperscaler GPU allocation.
regime_sensitivity: In a low-VIX regime (current: 16.9, normal), this call is maximum strength — growth momentum compounds and capital flows to high-conviction AI infrastructure plays. If VIX spikes above 22, the thesis remains intact but confidence drops from 4 to 3 because risk-off rotation would hit SMH and thematic growth positions regardless of NVDA fundamentals. The call does NOT reverse; it merely becomes a longer-duration hold waiting for volatility to recede.
watch_items: NVDA data centre revenue guidance misses consensus or declines sequentially in next earnings call — signals hyperscaler capex contraction
US export controls announce new restrictions on advanced GPU sales to China, Singapore, or other non-US governments — severs the sovereign AI demand thesis and reduces addressable market by 15-25%
